mission.title: atlantis: Support `atlantis import` subcommand
mission.description: Support `atlantis import` subcommand. Requested by 95+ users.

steps.title: Check current atlantis deployment
steps.description:
Verify your atlantis version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
helm list -n atlantis 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working atlantis installation.

steps.title: Review atlantis configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```
Terraform support importing existing resources in to the TF state file by way of the `terraform import` command. [Typical usage](https://www.terraform.io/docs/import/usage.html) looks like this:

1. Create resource that represents existing resource.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support `atlantis import` subcommand
steps.description:
## what

- introduce `import` workflow step
  - import step process `terraform import` and discard plan file, cause existing plan file cannot be used anymore.
- `import` workflow run `init` and `import` steps
- command runner can handle `atlantis import ADDRESS ID`
  - currently import run on a
```yaml
workflows:
  standard-with-custom-args:
    plan:
      steps:
      - init
      - run: /usr/local/bin/parse-custom-args.sh
      - plan
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support `atlantis import` subcommand" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## what

- introduce `import` workflow step
  - import step process `terraform import` and discard plan file, cause existing plan file cannot be used anymore.
- `import` workflow run `init` and `import` steps
- command runner can handle `atlantis import ADDRESS ID`
  - currently import run on a single project only. this is because we don't run import on multiple projects in the general use-case.
codeSnippets:
workflows:
  standard-with-custom-args:
    plan:
      steps:
      - init
      - run: /usr/local/bin/parse-custom-args.sh
      - plan

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with atlantis installed or the issue environment reproducible.
